title: Neo-Crusader assault armor
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Armor |image=[[File:Neo-Crusader_Shock_Troopers.jpg]] |name=Neo-Crusader assault armor |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Armor/Legends|Heavy armor]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |culture=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] |creators= |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners=[[Mandalore the Ultimate]] |locations= |cost=12,750 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|Credits]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' |value= |structure= |size= |color= |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight=65 kg |protection= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation=[[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusader]] }} {{Quote|Admiral! Those uniforms!''"
"''I know! Neo-Crusader shock troops!|[[Dallan Morvis]] and [[Saul Karath]]|[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]]}} The '''Neo-Crusader assault armor''', also known as '''Neo-Crusader shock armor''' or '''Mandalorian assault armor''' was a model of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] used during the [[Old Sith Wars]]. ==Description== The Neo-Crusader assault armor was a full body armor developed for the use of the [[Neo-Crusader shock trooper]]s during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. It was an upgraded and heavier version of the standard [[Neo-Crusader armor|Neo-Crusader light armor]] worn by most [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] during this conflict. However, contrary to the Neo-Crusader light armors that were virtually all identical, the assault armors were rather unique and customized for particular battlefield and environment requirements. [[File:Mandalorian_Assault_Armor.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[Atton Rand]] wearing a Mandalorian assault armor (without helmet).]] The assault armor was sealed and included enough oxygen to provide the wearer 24 hours of breathable air in the vacuum of space or any other hostile environment. The suit included a [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] with an internal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. In addition, it could be equipped with a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] but its use was rather limited due to the high weight of the armor. ==History== The Neo-Crusader assault armor was introduced during the Mandalorian Wars along with the first unit of Neo-Crusader shock troopers. These units were created by [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] in order to fill the gap of specialized troops generated by the uniformization of all the Mandalorian troops in one standardized army. In accordance, shock troopers did not follow the regular Neo-Crusader [[Order of Battle/Legends|order of battle]] and their armors did not comply with the regular three-color code. The Mandalorian assault armor saw wide use by Mandalorian elite frontline troops during the conflict. It was worn by Mandalorian shock troopers during the boarding of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] ship [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']] following the [[Battle of Serroco]]. An assault armor was also worn by Mandalore the Ultimate himself. Later during the war, a set of Neo-Crusader shock armor was recovered by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Battle of Myrkr (Mandalorian Wars)|Battle of Myrkr]] to examine by [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]].{{KOTORbackups|24}} Decades later, the Mandalorian assault armor was still in use during the [[Dark Wars]]. Nevertheless, this armor had become very rare and was only available at a high price. ==Behind the scenes== The Mandalorian assault armor first appeared in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' later provided more information about this armor and identified the Mandalorian armors worn by Mandalore the Ultimate and Neo-Crusader shock troopers in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series]] as Neo-Crusader assault armors. In the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', the Mandalorian assault armor was incorrectly depicted as the standard Neo-Crusader light armor.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]] *{{KOTORbackups|24}} {{Mo}} {{1stID|Neo-Crusader shock armor}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]] *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} {{1stID|Mandalorian assault armor}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{ID|Mandalorian assault armor}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{ID|Mandalorian assault armor}} *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' {{1stID|Neo-Crusader assault armor}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor17.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #17''}} [[Category:Mandalorian armor]]
